title: gluta malayana
intro: Gluta malayana, also known as the Bintangor tree, is a tropical plant native to Malaysia and other parts of Southeast Asia. It has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to lower cholesterol and blood pressure due to its high content of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Gluta malayana include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as an antioxidant to prevent cell damage. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infused into oil for its medicinal properties, with herbal preparations often involving the bark, leaves, or seeds of the tree.
